summary: Decent early Ultramatrix mix captures a mixed performance with a white‑hot B.I.O.D.T.L. rager, solid China/Rider and Truckin', but uneven vocals and occasional slip‑ups.
review: The recording is an early Healy matrix that sounds pleasant overall, though it shows the experimental mixing of late‑'86/early‑'87 with a few dropouts. Musically the show is uneven – Bobby’s vocals stumble on several numbers while the band delivers high points like the raging B.I.O.D.T.L., a tight China/Rider tag and an energetic Truckin', yet moments such as Ship of Fools suffer from forgotten verses.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed with both strong jams and noticeable performance lapses, while recording quality is generally good but shows early‑Healy mix quirks; combined weighted score yields ~3.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Early Healy Ultramatrix – pleasant and clear overall, but some reviewers note dropouts and the experimental nature of the mix making it less consistent than later FOB sources.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual set closing with Promised Land followed by an impromptu China/Rider tag; the first set ends on a high‑energy B.I.O.D.T.L. while the second set retains momentum despite occasional vocal and lyrical slips.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shows solid improvisational fire (especially on Truckin' and the Estimated jam) but flubs verses on Ship of Fools.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals are erratic this night – over‑the‑top frustration on songs like B.I.O.D.T.L. and other Bobby‑led numbers.
